summary: 2026-06-06 17:38:20 PDT - The branch makes the requested prose/bash-block changes, but the new pre-flight command uses a JSON field that the local `gh repo view` command does not support, so the remote `/close` path would abort even on correctly configured repositories. I could not rerun `bash tests/guard_test.sh` successfully because this read-only sandbox blocked its `mktemp`/temporary repo setup.

findings.title: Auto-merge preflight uses unsupported gh field
findings.claim: The added pre-flight uses `gh repo view --json autoMergeAllowed -q .autoMergeAllowed`, but `gh repo view` in this environment rejects `autoMergeAllowed` as an unknown JSON field. That means the remote merge path can fail before push/merge regardless of the repository's actual auto-merge setting, so AC4 is not reliably implemented.
findings.suggestion: Replace this with a supported API query, such as resolving `nameWithOwner` via `gh repo view` and querying the repo setting through `gh api`/GraphQL for `repository { autoMergeAllowed }`, then keep the same clear abort only when the returned value is actually false.
